description: claudemux hook bundle that drives the dispatcher's hook-based BUSY/idle signal. on-busy.sh sets /tmp/claude-idle/<sid>.busy on the FOUR events that transition a Claude session from idle → working (UserPromptSubmit, UserPromptExpansion, PreToolUse, PreCompact). on-stop.sh clears that marker, writes <sid>.last (Stop only), and touches the idle signal on the FOUR events that transition a session back to idle/gone (Stop, StopFailure, PostCompact, SessionEnd). on-session-start.sh keeps /tmp/teammate-<repo>.sid in sync when /clear rotates the session_id (without it, tm states/last/wait-idle all consult a dead sid). Hooks fire for every Claude session on the machine including the dispatcher itself; nothing waits on the dispatcher's own files, so the extra writes are harmless. Implementations are in on-busy.sh, on-stop.sh, and on-session-start.sh.
